subject: [PATCH v2 06/14] fuse: support buffer copying for kernel addresses

2026-04-02.patch_summary: This patch adds support for direct buffer copying in fuse, allowing it to work with kernel addresses instead of requiring page mapping and unmapping. This is a preparatory step for supporting pinned buffers in fuse-over-io-uring. The patch modifies the fuse_copy_init, fuse_copy_finish, fuse_copy_fill, and fuse_copy_do functions to handle both user-space and kernel-space buffer copying. It introduces a new 'is_kaddr' flag in the fuse_copy_state structure to indicate whether the buffer is a kernel address or not.
